summary: web3-secrets-detector exfiltrates host and environment data to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint
details: package.json declares a lifecycle script (line 30) that uses require('child_process'), require('os'), and require('https') to POST host information and process.env data to https://ddjidd564.github.io. scanner.js reinforces the same pattern: it imports fs/os/https/child_process, builds a payload object containing hostname, cwd, version, and the output of `whoami`, then sends it via https.request/https.get to the same hardcoded https://ddjidd564.github.io endpoint (referenced at lines 161 and 371). The destination is a personal GitHub Pages site with no relation to any legitimate publisher, the package's stated purpose ("web3 secrets detector") provides cover for filesystem enumeration, and the system-fingerprint payload (hostname + cwd + whoami + env) is the canonical credential/host reconnaissance shape. Installing or invoking this package causes the installer's machine identity and environment to be transmitted to the attacker.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
